"""Shared parser, serializer, and data types for objective roadmap operations. This module provides: - Data types: RoadmapNodeStatus, RoadmapNode, RoadmapPhase - Parsing: parse_roadmap() (v2 frontmatter only) - Frontmatter: validate, parse, group, update - Utilities: compute_summary(), find_next_node(), serialize_phases() Previously split across objective_roadmap_shared.py and objective_roadmap_frontmatter.py in the erk package. Consolidated here to eliminate the circular dependency between erk_shared and erk. """ import re from collections.abc import Mapping from dataclasses import dataclass, replace from typing import Literal, cast import yaml from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( extract_raw_metadata_blocks, parse_metadata_block_body, ) from erk_shared.naming import make_unique_slug, slugify_node_description RoadmapNodeStatus = Literal["pending", "planning", "done", "in_progress", "blocked", "skipped"] @dataclass(frozen=True) class RoadmapNode: """A single node in a roadmap phase.""" id: str description: str status: RoadmapNodeStatus plan: str | None # None or "#123" (plan issue number) pr: str | None # None or "#456" (landed PR number) depends_on: tuple[str, ...] | None # None = not specified, () = no deps slug: str | None # None = not yet generated, or kebab-case slug @dataclass(frozen=True) class RoadmapPhase: """A phase in the objective roadmap.""" number: int suffix: str # Letter suffix, e.g. "A" for "Phase 1A", "" for "Phase 1" name: str nodes: list[RoadmapNode] # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Frontmatter validation and parsing # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def validate_roadmap_frontmatter( data: Mapping[str, object], ) -> tuple[list[RoadmapNode] | None, list[str]]: """Validate parsed frontmatter against the roadmap schema. Args: data: Parsed YAML dictionary. Returns: Tuple of (steps, errors). If validation succeeds, errors is empty. If validation fails, steps is None. """ errors: list[str] = [] # Validate schema_version schema_version = data.get("schema_version") if schema_version is None: errors.append("Missing required field: schema_version") return None, errors if schema_version not in ("2", "3", "4"): errors.append(f"Unsupported schema_version: {schema_version}") return None, errors # Validate items list — accept "nodes" (v3) or "steps" (v2) if "nodes" in data: steps_data = data["nodes"] elif "steps" in data: steps_data = data["steps"] else: errors.append("Missing required field: nodes (or steps for v2)") return None, errors if not isinstance(steps_data, list): errors.append("Field 'steps' must be a list") return None, errors # Parse each step steps: list[RoadmapNode] = [] for i, step_data in enumerate(steps_data): if not isinstance(step_data, dict): errors.append(f"Step {i} is not a mapping") return None, errors step_dict = cast(dict[str, object], step_data) # Check required fields for field in ("id", "description", "status"): if field not in step_dict: errors.append(f"Step {i} missing required field: {field}") return None, errors step_id = step_dict["id"] description = step_dict["description"] status = step_dict["status"] raw_plan = step_dict.get("plan") raw_pr = step_dict.get("pr") # Validate types if not isinstance(step_id, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'id' must be a string") return None, errors if not isinstance(description, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'description' must be a string") return None, errors if not isinstance(status, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'status' must be a string") return None, errors if status not in {"pending", "planning", "done", "in_progress", "blocked", "skipped"}: valid_statuses = "pending, planning, done, in_progress, blocked, skipped" errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'status' must be one of: {valid_statuses}") return None, errors if raw_plan is not None and not isinstance(raw_plan, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'plan' must be a string or null") return None, errors if raw_pr is not None and not isinstance(raw_pr, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'pr' must be a string or null") return None, errors raw_depends_on = step_dict.get("depends_on") depends_on: tuple[str, ...] | None = None if raw_depends_on is not None: if not isinstance(raw_depends_on, list): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'depends_on' must be a list or null") return None, errors for j, item in enumerate(raw_depends_on): if not isinstance(item, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'depends_on' item {j} must be a string") return None, errors depends_on = tuple(cast(list[str], raw_depends_on)) raw_slug = step_dict.get("slug") if raw_slug is not None and not isinstance(raw_slug, str): errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'slug' must be a string or null") return None, errors steps.append( RoadmapNode( id=step_id, description=description, status=cast(RoadmapNodeStatus, status), plan=raw_plan, pr=raw_pr, depends_on=depends_on, slug=raw_slug, ) ) # Auto-generate slugs for nodes that don't have them (lazy migration) seen_slugs: set[str] = set() # First pass: collect existing slugs for step in steps: if step.slug is not None: seen_slugs.add(step.slug) # Second pass: generate missing slugs migrated_steps: list[RoadmapNode] = [] for step in steps: if step.slug is not None: migrated_steps.append(step) else: generated = slugify_node_description(step.description) unique_slug = make_unique_slug(generated, seen_slugs) seen_slugs.add(unique_slug) migrated_steps.append(replace(step, slug=unique_slug)) return migrated_steps, errors def parse_roadmap_frontmatter(block_content: str) -> list[RoadmapNode] | None: """Parse YAML from objective-roadmap metadata block content. Only supports ``
`` + code block format (v2). Args: block_content: Raw content from inside the metadata block (between the HTML comment markers) Returns: Flat list of steps if valid YAML found, None otherwise """ if not block_content.strip().startswith("
"): return None try: data = parse_metadata_block_body(block_content) except ValueError: return None steps, _errors = validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data) return steps def render_roadmap_block_inner(nodes: list[RoadmapNode]) -> str: """Render roadmap nodes as
wrapped YAML code block. This produces the same format as other metadata blocks (plan-header, objective-header), making roadmap blocks collapsible and well-formatted on GitHub. Args: nodes: Flat list of roadmap nodes to render. Returns: Inner content for an objective-roadmap metadata block, wrapped in ``
`` with a YAML code block. """ any_has_depends_on = any(s.depends_on is not None for s in nodes) node_dicts: list[dict[str, object]] = [] for s in nodes: node_dict: dict[str, object] = { "id": s.id, "slug": s.slug, "description": s.description, "status": s.status, "plan": s.plan, "pr": s.pr, } if any_has_depends_on: node_dict["depends_on"] = list(s.depends_on) if s.depends_on is not None else [] node_dicts.append(node_dict) data: dict[str, object] = { "schema_version": "4", "nodes": node_dicts, } yaml_content = yaml.safe_dump( data, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True, sort_keys=False, ) yaml_content = yaml_content.rstrip("\n") return ( "
\n" "objective-roadmap\n" "\n" "```yaml\n" f"{yaml_content}\n" "```\n" "\n" "
" ) def group_nodes_by_phase(nodes: list[RoadmapNode]) -> list[RoadmapPhase]: """Reconstruct RoadmapPhase objects from node ID prefixes. Phase membership is derived by convention: - "1.1", "1.2" → phase 1 - "2A.1", "2A.2" → phase 2A - "3.1" → phase 3 Phase names are NOT stored in frontmatter, so this returns phases with placeholder names. Callers that need phase names must extract them from markdown headers. Args: nodes: Flat list of nodes Returns: List of RoadmapPhase objects grouped by ID prefix """ # Group nodes by parsed phase key (number, suffix) to merge equivalent phases. # Raw phase_id strings like "1" and "1.2" both resolve to phase (1, ""), # so we group by the resolved key rather than the raw string. phase_map: dict[tuple[int, str], list[RoadmapNode]] = {} for node in nodes: # Extract phase identifier from node ID # "1.1" → "1", "2A.1" → "2A", "1.2.3" → "1.2" # "1", "2", "A" (no dot) → default to phase (1, "") if "." not in node.id: phase_key = (1, "") else: phase_id = node.id.rsplit(".", 1)[0] match = re.match(r"^(\d+)([A-Z]*)", phase_id) if match: phase_key = (int(match.group(1)), match.group(2)) else: # Non-numeric phase ID (e.g., "A") — assign to phase 1 phase_key = (1, "") if phase_key not in phase_map: phase_map[phase_key] = [] phase_map[phase_key].append(node) # Convert to RoadmapPhase objects phases: list[RoadmapPhase] = [] for (phase_number, phase_suffix), phase_steps in phase_map.items(): phase_name = f"Phase {phase_number}{phase_suffix}" phases.append( RoadmapPhase( number=phase_number, suffix=phase_suffix, name=phase_name, nodes=phase_steps, ) ) # Sort phases by number, then suffix phases.sort(key=lambda p: (p.number, p.suffix)) return phases def update_node_in_frontmatter( block_content: str, node_id: str, *, plan: str | None, pr: str | None, status: RoadmapNodeStatus | None, ) -> str | None: """Update a node's plan/PR fields (and optionally status) in frontmatter YAML. Args: block_content: Raw content from metadata block node_id: Node ID to update (e.g., "1.1") plan: New plan value. None=preserve existing, ""=clear, "#6464"=set. pr: New PR value. None=preserve existing, ""=clear, "#123"=set. status: Explicit status to set, or None to infer from resolved values. Returns: Updated block content with modified YAML, or None if node not found """ steps = parse_roadmap_frontmatter(block_content) if steps is None: return None # Find and update the node found = False updated_steps: list[RoadmapNode] = [] for step in steps: if step.id == node_id: # Resolve PR: None=preserve, ""=clear, "#123"=set if pr is None: resolved_pr = step.pr elif pr: resolved_pr = pr else: resolved_pr = None # Resolve plan: None=preserve, ""=clear, "#6464"=set if plan is None: resolved_plan = step.plan else: resolved_plan = plan or None # Determine status: explicit > infer from resolved values > preserve new_status: RoadmapNodeStatus if status is not None: new_status = status elif resolved_pr: new_status = cast(RoadmapNodeStatus, "in_progress") elif resolved_plan: new_status = cast(RoadmapNodeStatus, "in_progress") else: new_status = step.status # preserve existing status updated_steps.append( replace(step, status=new_status, plan=resolved_plan, pr=resolved_pr) ) found = True else: updated_steps.append(step) if not found: return None return render_roadmap_block_inner(updated_steps) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Roadmap parsing (frontmatter-first, table fallback) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def enrich_phase_names(body: str, phases: list[RoadmapPhase]) -> list[RoadmapPhase]: """Extract phase names from markdown headers and enrich phase objects. Frontmatter doesn't store phase names, so we extract them from markdown headers like "### Phase 1: Planning". Args: body: Full objective body with markdown headers phases: List of phases with placeholder names Returns: List of phases with actual names from markdown headers """ # Build map of phase identifiers to names from markdown phase_pattern = re.compile( r"^###\s+Phase\s+(\d+)([A-Z]?):\s*(.+?)(?:\s+\(\d+\s+PR\))?$", re.MULTILINE ) phase_name_map: dict[tuple[int, str], str] = {} for match in phase_pattern.finditer(body): number = int(match.group(1)) suffix = match.group(2) name = match.group(3).strip() phase_name_map[(number, suffix)] = name # Enrich phases with actual names enriched_phases: list[RoadmapPhase] = [] for phase in phases: key = (phase.number, phase.suffix) if key in phase_name_map: # Replace placeholder name with actual name from markdown enriched_phases.append(replace(phase, name=phase_name_map[key])) else: # Keep placeholder name if no markdown header found enriched_phases.append(phase) return enriched_phases def parse_v2_roadmap(body: str) -> tuple[list[RoadmapPhase], list[str]] | None: """Parse roadmap strictly from v2 ``
`` format. Unlike :func:`parse_roadmap`, this function does **not** fall back to legacy table parsing. It returns ``None`` when the body does not contain a v2-format ``objective-roadmap`` metadata block, signalling the caller that the objective uses a legacy format. Returns: ``(phases, validation_errors)`` on success, or ``None`` when the body is not in v2 format. """ raw_blocks = extract_raw_metadata_blocks(body) matching_blocks = [block for block in raw_blocks if block.key == "objective-roadmap"] if not matching_blocks: return None roadmap_block = matching_blocks[0] if not roadmap_block.body.strip().startswith("
"): return None data = parse_metadata_block_body(roadmap_block.body) if data.get("schema_version") not in ("2", "3", "4"): return None steps, errors = validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data) if steps is None: return None phases = group_nodes_by_phase(steps) phases = enrich_phase_names(body, phases) return (phases, errors) _LEGACY_FORMAT_ERROR = ( "This objective uses a legacy format that is no longer supported. " "To migrate, open Claude Code and use /erk:objective-create to " "recreate this objective with the same content." ) def parse_roadmap(body: str) -> tuple[list[RoadmapPhase], list[str]]: """Parse roadmap from v2 YAML frontmatter in objective-roadmap metadata block. Returns: (phases, validation_errors) """ raw_blocks = extract_raw_metadata_blocks(body) matching_blocks = [block for block in raw_blocks if block.key == "objective-roadmap"] if matching_blocks: roadmap_block = matching_blocks[0] try: steps = parse_roadmap_frontmatter(roadmap_block.body) except ValueError: steps = None if steps is not None: phases = group_nodes_by_phase(steps) phases = enrich_phase_names(body, phases) return (phases, []) # No valid v2 metadata block found — legacy format or missing return ([], [_LEGACY_FORMAT_ERROR]) def _format_depends_on(depends_on: tuple[str, ...] | None) -> str: """Format depends_on for table display. None or empty tuple renders as ``-``, non-empty tuple as comma-separated IDs. """ if not depends_on: return "-" return ", ".join(depends_on) def render_roadmap_tables(phases: list[RoadmapPhase]) -> str: """Render phases as markdown tables matching the objective-body display format. Format per phase: ### Phase {number}{suffix}: {name} ({N} PR) | Node | Description | Status | Plan | PR | |------|-------------|--------|------|----| | {id} | {desc} | {status} | {plan} | {pr} | When any node across all phases has ``depends_on`` specified, a "Depends On" column is inserted between Description and Status. Status display: underscores are replaced with hyphens (in_progress → in-progress). Null values render as ``-``. """ any_has_depends_on = any( step.depends_on is not None for phase in phases for step in phase.nodes ) sections: list[str] = [] for phase in phases: pr_count = sum(1 for step in phase.nodes if step.pr is not None) header = f"### Phase {phase.number}{phase.suffix}: {phase.name} ({pr_count} PR)" rows: list[str] = [] if any_has_depends_on: rows.append("| Node | Description | Depends On | Status | Plan | PR |") rows.append("|------|-------------|------------|--------|------|----|") else: rows.append("| Node | Description | Status | Plan | PR |") rows.append("|------|-------------|--------|------|----|") for step in phase.nodes: status_display = step.status.replace("_", "-") cells = [ step.id, step.description, ] if any_has_depends_on: cells.append(_format_depends_on(step.depends_on)) cells.extend( [ status_display, step.plan if step.plan is not None else "-", step.pr if step.pr is not None else "-", ] ) rows.append("| " + " | ".join(cells) + " |") sections.append(header + "\n" + "\n".join(rows)) return "\n\n".join(sections) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Utility functions # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def compute_summary(phases: list[RoadmapPhase]) -> dict[str, int]: """Compute summary statistics from phases.""" total = 0 pending = 0 planning = 0 done = 0 in_progress = 0 blocked = 0 skipped = 0 for phase in phases: for step in phase.nodes: total += 1 if step.status == "pending": pending += 1 elif step.status == "planning": planning += 1 elif step.status == "done": done += 1 elif step.status == "in_progress": in_progress += 1 elif step.status == "blocked": blocked += 1 elif step.status == "skipped": skipped += 1 return { "total_nodes": total, "pending": pending, "planning": planning, "done": done, "in_progress": in_progress, "blocked": blocked, "skipped": skipped, } def serialize_phases(phases: list[RoadmapPhase]) -> list[dict[str, object]]: """Convert phases to JSON-serializable format.""" return [ { "number": phase.number, "suffix": phase.suffix, "name": phase.name, "nodes": [ { "id": step.id, "slug": step.slug, "description": step.description, "status": step.status, "plan": step.plan, "pr": step.pr, "depends_on": list(step.depends_on) if step.depends_on is not None else None, } for step in phase.nodes ], } for phase in phases ] def find_next_node(phases: list[RoadmapPhase]) -> dict[str, str] | None: """Find the first pending step in phase order.""" for phase in phases: for step in phase.nodes: if step.status == "pending": return { "id": step.id, "slug": step.slug or "", "description": step.description, "phase": phase.name, } return None # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Roadmap table markers # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START = "" ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END = "" def wrap_roadmap_tables_with_markers(content: str) -> str: """Wrap the roadmap section (phase headers + tables) with HTML comment markers. Finds the first ``### Phase N:`` header through the end of the last table and wraps the entire range with ```` / ````. If markers already exist, replaces them in-place. If no phase headers are found, returns content unchanged. Args: content: Objective markdown content (typically the objective-body comment). Returns: Content with roadmap section wrapped in markers. """ # Remove any existing markers first content = content.replace(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START + "\n", "") content = content.replace("\n" + ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END, "") content = content.replace(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START, "") content = content.replace(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END, "") # Find all phase headers phase_pattern = re.compile(r"^###\s+Phase\s+\d+[A-Z]?:\s*.+$", re.MULTILINE) phase_matches = list(phase_pattern.finditer(content)) if not phase_matches: return content # Start of the roadmap section is the first phase header roadmap_start = phase_matches[0].start() # End is after the last table row following the last phase header last_phase_end = phase_matches[-1].end() remaining = content[last_phase_end:] # Find the last table row (| ... |) after the last phase header table_row_pattern = re.compile(r"^\|.+\|$", re.MULTILINE) last_row_end = last_phase_end for match in table_row_pattern.finditer(remaining): last_row_end = last_phase_end + match.end() roadmap_end = last_row_end # Extract the roadmap section and wrap it before = content[:roadmap_start] roadmap = content[roadmap_start:roadmap_end] after = content[roadmap_end:] return f"{before}{ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START}\n{roadmap}\n{ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END}{after}" def extract_roadmap_table_section(text: str) -> tuple[str, int, int] | None: """Extract the roadmap table section bounded by markers. Args: text: Full text that may contain roadmap table markers. Returns: Tuple of (section_content, start_offset, end_offset) if markers found, None otherwise. """ start_idx = text.find(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START) if start_idx == -1: return None end_idx = text.find(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END, start_idx) if end_idx == -1: return None content_start = start_idx + len(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_START) section = text[content_start:end_idx] return (section, start_idx, end_idx + len(ROADMAP_TABLE_MARKER_END))

1→"""Naming utilities for filenames and worktree names.
     2→
     3→This module provides pure utility functions for transforming titles and names
     4→into sanitized, filesystem-safe identifiers. All functions are pure (no I/O)
     5→and follow LBYL patterns.
     6→
     7→Functions that require git operations accept a git_ops parameter via dependency
     8→injection to maintain separation from I/O concerns.
     9→"""
    10→
    11→import re
    12→import unicodedata
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass
    14→from datetime import datetime
    15→from pathlib import Path
    16→
    17→_SAFE_COMPONENT_RE = re.compile(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_/-]+")
    18→
    19→# Date suffix format for plan-derived worktree names: -YY-MM-DD-HHMM
    20→WORKTREE_DATE_SUFFIX_FORMAT = "%y-%m-%d-%H%M"
    21→
    22→# Branch timestamp suffix format: -MM-DD-HHMM (appended after truncation)
    23→BRANCH_TIMESTAMP_SUFFIX_FORMAT = "%m-%d-%H%M"
    24→
    25→# Regex pattern to detect existing timestamp suffix (MM-DD-HHMM)
    26→_TIMESTAMP_SUFFIX_PATTERN = re.compile(r"-\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{4}$")
    27→
    28→# Default/fallback titles that extractors return when no real title exists.
    29→# These must be rejected so agents provide meaningful plan titles.
    30→_FALLBACK_PLAN_TITLES = {"Untitled Plan", "Implementation Plan"}
    31→
    32→
    33→def has_timestamp_suffix(name: str) -> bool:
    34→    """Check if a name already ends with a timestamp suffix (-MM-DD-HHMM).
    35→
    36→    Args:
    37→        name: Branch or worktree name to check
    38→
    39→    Returns:
    40→        True if name ends with timestamp suffix pattern, False otherwise
    41→
    42→    Examples:
    43→        >>> has_timestamp_suffix("42-feature-01-15-1430")
    44→        True
    45→        >>> has_timestamp_suffix("42-feature")
    46→        False
    47→        >>> has_timestamp_suffix("42-feature-branch")
    48→        False
    49→    """
    50→    return _TIMESTAMP_SUFFIX_PATTERN.search(name) is not None
    51→
    52→
    53→def format_branch_timestamp_suffix(dt: datetime) -> str:
    54→    """Format a datetime as a branch timestamp suffix.
    55→
    56→    Returns a suffix in the format -MM-DD-HHMM to be appended to branch names.
    57→
    58→    Args:
    59→        dt: Datetime to format
    60→
    61→    Returns:
    62→        Formatted suffix string (e.g., "-01-15-1430")
    63→
    64→    Examples:
    65→        >>> from datetime import datetime
    66→        >>> format_branch_timestamp_suffix(datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30))
    67→        "-01-15-1430"
    68→        >>> format_branch_timestamp_suffix(datetime(2024, 12, 31, 23, 59))
    69→        "-12-31-2359"
    70→    """
    71→    return f"-{dt.strftime(BRANCH_TIMESTAMP_SUFFIX_FORMAT)}"
    72→
    73→
    74→_OBJECTIVE_SLUG_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(-[a-z0-9]+)*$")
    75→
    76→# Node slug constraints
    77→_NODE_SLUG_MIN_LENGTH = 2
    78→_NODE_SLUG_MAX_LENGTH = 30
    79→
    80→# Common filler words to strip in deterministic slug generation
    81→_FILLER_WORDS = frozenset({
    82→    "a", "an", "and", "the", "to", "for", "of", "in", "on", "with",
    83→    "from", "by", "into", "this", "that", "is", "are", "be",
    84→})
    85→
    86→# Plan title constraints
    87→_PLAN_TITLE_MIN_LENGTH = 5
    88→_PLAN_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH = 100
    89→
    90→
    91→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    92→class ValidPlanTitle:
    93→    """Validation success for a plan title.
    94→
    95→    Attributes:
    96→        title: The validated title value.
    97→    """
    98→
    99→    title: str
   100→
   101→
   102→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   103→class InvalidPlanTitle:
   104→    """Validation failure for a plan title.
   105→
   106→    Attributes:
   107→        raw_title: The original title value that failed validation.
   108→        reason: A short description of why validation failed.
   109→    """
   110→
   111→    raw_title: str
   112→    reason: str
   113→
   114→    @property
   115→    def error_type(self) -> str:
   116→        return "invalid-plan-title"
   117→
   118→    @property
   119→    def message(self) -> str:
   120→        """Full error message with rules, actual value, and examples.
   121→
   122→        Designed so an agent receiving this message can self-correct.
   123→        """
   124→        return (
   125→            f"Invalid plan title: {self.reason}\n"
   126→            f"  Actual value: {self.raw_title!r}\n"
   127→            f"  Rules:\n"
   128→            f"    - {_PLAN_TITLE_MIN_LENGTH}-{_PLAN_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH} characters\n"
   129→            f"    - Must contain at least one alphabetic character\n"
   130→            f"    - Must retain meaningful content after sanitization\n"
   131→            f"      (emojis, special characters, and accents are stripped)\n"
   132→            f"  Valid examples: Add User Authentication,"
   133→            f" Refactor Gateway Layer\n"
   134→            f"  Invalid examples: \U0001f680\U0001f389, 123, #!@, Plan"
   135→        )
   136→
   137→
   138→def validate_plan_title(title: str) -> ValidPlanTitle | InvalidPlanTitle:
   139→    """Validate a plan title against minimum content requirements.
   140→
   141→    This is an agent-facing validation gate. It ensures plan titles have enough
   142→    meaningful content before they are used to generate filenames, branch names,
   143→    or issue titles. Human-facing paths (like generate_filename_from_title) can
   144→    still transform arbitrary input silently.
   145→
   146→    Returns ValidPlanTitle on success, or InvalidPlanTitle describing the failure.
   147→
   148→    Args:
   149→        title: The plan title string to validate.
   150→
   151→    Returns:
   152→        ValidPlanTitle if valid, InvalidPlanTitle if invalid.
   153→
   154→    Examples:
   155→        >>> validate_plan_title("Add User Authentication")
   156→        ValidPlanTitle(title='Add User Authentication')
   157→        >>> validate_plan_title("")
   158→        InvalidPlanTitle(raw_title='', reason='...')
   159→        >>> validate_plan_title("🚀🎉")
   160→        InvalidPlanTitle(raw_title='🚀🎉', reason='...')
   161→    """
   162→    stripped = title.strip()
   163→
   164→    if not stripped:
   165→        return InvalidPlanTitle(raw_title=title, reason="Title is empty or whitespace-only")
   166→
   167→    if len(stripped) < _PLAN_TITLE_MIN_LENGTH:
   168→        return InvalidPlanTitle(
   169→            raw_title=title,
   170→            reason=f"Too short ({len(stripped)} characters, minimum {_PLAN_TITLE_MIN_LENGTH})",
   171→        )
   172→
   173→    if len(stripped) > _PLAN_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH:
   174→        return InvalidPlanTitle(
   175→            raw_title=title,
   176→            reason=f"Too long ({len(stripped)} characters, maximum {_PLAN_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH})",
   177→        )
   178→
   179→    if not any(c.isalpha() for c in stripped):
   180→        return InvalidPlanTitle(
   181→            raw_title=title,
   182→            reason="Must contain at least one alphabetic character",
   183→        )
   184→
   185→    if stripped in _FALLBACK_PLAN_TITLES:
   186→        return InvalidPlanTitle(
   187→            raw_title=title,
   188→            reason=f"'{stripped}' is a default fallback title, not a descriptive plan title",
   189→        )
   190→
   191→    # Check that sanitization retains meaningful content.
   192→    # generate_filename_from_title strips emojis, accents, and special chars.
   193→    # If the result is "plan.md" (the fallback), the title has no usable content.
   194→    filename = generate_filename_from_title(stripped)
   195→    if filename == "plan.md":
   196→        return InvalidPlanTitle(
   197→            raw_title=title,
   198→            reason="No usable content after sanitization (only emojis or special characters)",
   199→        )
   200→
   201→    return ValidPlanTitle(title=stripped)
   202→
   203→
   204→# Worktree name constraints
   205→_WORKTREE_NAME_MAX_LENGTH = 31
   206→
   207→
   208→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   209→class ValidWorktreeName:
   210→    """Validation success for a worktree name."""
   211→
   212→    name: str
   213→
   214→
   215→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   216→class InvalidWorktreeName:
   217→    """Validation failure for a worktree name."""
   218→
   219→    raw_name: str
   220→    reason: str
   221→    diagnostics: list[str]
   222→
   223→    @property
   224→    def error_type(self) -> str:
   225→        return "invalid-worktree-name"
   226→
   227→    def format_message(self) -> str:
   228→        """Full error message with rules and diagnostics for agent self-correction.
   229→
   230→        Uses method (not property) because it builds a multi-line string.
   231→        """
   232→        diag_lines = "\n".join(f"    - {d}" for d in self.diagnostics)
   233→        return (
   234→            f"Invalid worktree name: {self.reason}\n"
   235→            f"  Actual value: {self.raw_name!r}\n"
   236→            f"  Diagnostics:\n{diag_lines}\n"
   237→            f"  Rules:\n"
   238→            f"    - Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only [a-z0-9-]\n"
   239→            f"    - No underscores (use hyphens)\n"
   240→            f"    - No consecutive hyphens\n"
   241→            f"    - No leading/trailing hyphens\n"
   242→            f"    - Maximum {_WORKTREE_NAME_MAX_LENGTH} characters\n"
   243→            f"  Valid examples: add-auth-feature, fix-bug-123\n"
   244→            f"  Invalid examples: Add_Auth, my__feature, --name"
   245→        )
   246→
   247→
   248→def _diagnose_worktree_name(name: str) -> list[str]:
   249→    """Identify specific validation failures in a worktree name."""
   250→    issues: list[str] = []
   251→    if name != name.lower():
   252→        issues.append("Contains uppercase letters (must be lowercase)")
   253→    if "_" in name:
   254→        issues.append("Contains underscores (use hyphens instead)")
   255→    if re.search(r"[^a-z0-9-]", name):
   256→        bad_chars = sorted(set(re.findall(r"[^a-z0-9-]", name)))
   257→        issues.append(f"Contains invalid characters: {bad_chars}")
   258→    if "--" in name:
   259→        issues.append("Contains consecutive hyphens")
   260→    if name.startswith("-") or name.endswith("-"):
   261→        issues.append("Has leading or trailing hyphens")
   262→    if len(name) > _WORKTREE_NAME_MAX_LENGTH:
   263→        issues.append(f"Too long ({len(name)} characters, maximum {_WORKTREE_NAME_MAX_LENGTH})")
   264→    return issues
   265→
   266→
   267→def validate_worktree_name(name: str) -> ValidWorktreeName | InvalidWorktreeName:
   268→    """Validate a worktree name against sanitization rules.
   269→
   270→    Agent-facing validation gate. Accepts names that are already clean
   271→    (would pass through sanitize_worktree_name() unchanged). Rejects names
   272→    that would be silently transformed.
   273→
   274→    Names with timestamp suffixes (-MM-DD-HHMM) are treated as idempotent
   275→    and pass validation (they've already been sanitized).
   276→    """
   277→    stripped = name.strip()
   278→
   279→    if not stripped:
   280→        return InvalidWorktreeName(
   281→            raw_name=name, reason="Empty or whitespace-only", diagnostics=["Name is empty"]
   282→        )
   283→
   284→    # Timestamp-suffixed names are idempotent — already sanitized
   285→    if has_timestamp_suffix(stripped):
   286→        return ValidWorktreeName(name=stripped)
   287→
   288→    # Check if sanitization would change the name
   289→    sanitized = sanitize_worktree_name(stripped)
   290→    if sanitized == stripped:
   291→        return ValidWorktreeName(name=stripped)
   292→
   293→    # Name would be transformed — diagnose why
   294→    diagnostics = _diagnose_worktree_name(stripped)
   295→    if not diagnostics:
   296→        diagnostics = [f"Would be transformed to: {sanitized!r}"]
   297→
   298→    return InvalidWorktreeName(
   299→        raw_name=name,
   300→        reason="Name would be silently transformed by sanitization",
   301→        diagnostics=diagnostics,
   302→    )
   303→
   304→
   305→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   306→class ValidObjectiveSlug:
   307→    """Validation success for an objective slug.
   308→
   309→    Attributes:
   310→        slug: The validated slug value.
   311→    """
   312→
   313→    slug: str
   314→
   315→
   316→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   317→class InvalidObjectiveSlug:
   318→    """Validation failure for an objective slug.
   319→
   320→    Attributes:
   321→        raw_slug: The original slug value that failed validation.
   322→        reason: A short description of why validation failed.
   323→    """
   324→
   325→    raw_slug: str
   326→    reason: str
   327→
   328→    @property
   329→    def message(self) -> str:
   330→        """Full error message with pattern, rules, actual value, and examples.
   331→
   332→        Designed so an agent receiving this message can self-correct.
   333→        """
   334→        return (
   335→            f"Invalid objective slug: {self.reason}\n"
   336→            f"  Actual value: {self.raw_slug!r}\n"
   337→            f"  Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(-[a-z0-9]+)*$\n"
   338→            f"  Rules:\n"
   339→            f"    - 3-40 characters\n"
   340→            f"    - Lowercase letters and digits only\n"
   341→            f"    - Must start with a letter\n"
   342→            f"    - Hyphens allowed between words (no consecutive hyphens)\n"
   343→            f"  Valid examples: build-auth-system, refactor-gateway, add-dark-mode\n"
   344→            f"  Invalid examples: Build-Auth, 123-start, my--slug, ab"
   345→        )
   346→
   347→
   348→def validate_objective_slug(slug: str) -> ValidObjectiveSlug | InvalidObjectiveSlug:
   349→    """Validate an objective slug against the required format.
   350→
   351→    Returns ValidObjectiveSlug on success, or InvalidObjectiveSlug describing the failure.
   352→
   353→    Args:
   354→        slug: The slug string to validate.
   355→
   356→    Returns:
   357→        ValidObjectiveSlug if valid, InvalidObjectiveSlug if invalid.
   358→
   359→    Examples:
   360→        >>> validate_objective_slug("build-auth-system")  # valid
   361→        ValidObjectiveSlug(slug='build-auth-system')
   362→        >>> validate_objective_slug("AB")  # invalid
   363→        InvalidObjectiveSlug(raw_slug='AB', reason='...')
   364→    """
   365→    if len(slug) < 3:
   366→        return InvalidObjectiveSlug(raw_slug=slug, reason="Too short (minimum 3 characters)")
   367→    if len(slug) > 40:
   368→        return InvalidObjectiveSlug(raw_slug=slug, reason="Too long (maximum 40 characters)")
   369→    if _OBJECTIVE_SLUG_PATTERN.match(slug) is None:
   370→        return InvalidObjectiveSlug(raw_slug=slug, reason="Does not match required pattern")
   371→    return ValidObjectiveSlug(slug=slug)
   372→

   417→def validate_node_slug(slug: str) -> ValidNodeSlug | InvalidNodeSlug:
   418→    """Validate a node slug against the required format.
   419→
   420→    Node slugs follow the same pattern as objective slugs but with
   421→    different length constraints (2-30 characters).
   422→
   423→    Args:
   424→        slug: The slug string to validate.
   425→
   426→    Returns:
   427→        ValidNodeSlug if valid, InvalidNodeSlug if invalid.
   428→    """
   429→    if len(slug) < _NODE_SLUG_MIN_LENGTH:
   430→        return InvalidNodeSlug(
   431→            raw_slug=slug,
   432→            reason=f"Too short (minimum {_NODE_SLUG_MIN_LENGTH} characters)",
   433→        )
   434→    if len(slug) > _NODE_SLUG_MAX_LENGTH:
   435→        return InvalidNodeSlug(
   436→            raw_slug=slug,
   437→            reason=f"Too long (maximum {_NODE_SLUG_MAX_LENGTH} characters)",
   438→        )
   439→    if _OBJECTIVE_SLUG_PATTERN.match(slug) is None:
   440→        return InvalidNodeSlug(raw_slug=slug, reason="Does not match required pattern")
   441→    return ValidNodeSlug(slug=slug)

   444→def slugify_node_description(description: str) -> str:
   445→    """Generate a deterministic slug from a node description.
   446→
   447→    Deterministic fallback for when LLM is unavailable. Transforms a
   448→    description into a kebab-case slug by:
   449→    1. Lowercasing
   450→    2. Stripping filler words
   451→    3. Taking first 4 meaningful words
   452→    4. Hyphenating
   453→    5. Truncating to 30 characters
   454→
   455→    Args:
   456→        description: Node description to slugify.
   457→
   458→    Returns:
   459→        A kebab-case slug, max 30 characters.
   460→    """
   461→    lowered = description.strip().lower()
   462→    # Replace non-alphanumeric with spaces for splitting
   463→    cleaned = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", " ", lowered)
   464→    words = cleaned.split()
   465→    # Strip filler words
   466→    meaningful = [w for w in words if w not in _FILLER_WORDS]
   467→    if not meaningful:
   468→        meaningful = words[:4] if words else ["node"]
   469→    # Take first 4 words
   470→    slug_words = meaningful[:4]
   471→    slug = "-".join(slug_words)
   472→    # Truncate to 30 chars, strip trailing hyphen
   473→    if len(slug) > _NODE_SLUG_MAX_LENGTH:
   474→        slug = slug[:_NODE_SLUG_MAX_LENGTH].rstrip("-")
   475→    return slug
   476→
   477→
   478→def make_unique_slug(slug: str, existing_slugs: set[str]) -> str:
   479→    """Ensure a slug is unique within a set by appending a numeric suffix.
   480→
   481→    If the slug already exists, appends -2, -3, etc. until unique.
   482→
   483→    Args:
   484→        slug: The base slug to check.
   485→        existing_slugs: Set of slugs already in use.
   486→
   487→    Returns:
   488→        A unique slug, either the original or with a numeric suffix.
   489→    """
   490→    if slug not in existing_slugs:
   491→        return slug
   492→    counter = 2
   493→    while True:
   494→        candidate = f"{slug}-{counter}"
   495→        if candidate not in existing_slugs:
   496→            return candidate
   497→        counter += 1
   498→

   500→def sanitize_worktree_name(name: str) -> str:
   501→    """Sanitize a worktree name for use as a directory name.
   502→
   503→    - If name already has timestamp suffix (-MM-DD-HHMM), returns as-is (idempotent)
   504→    - Lowercases input
   505→    - Replaces underscores with hyphens
   506→    - Replaces characters outside `[A-Za-z0-9-]` with `-`
   507→    - Collapses consecutive `-`
   508→    - Strips leading/trailing `-`
   509→    - Truncates to 31 characters maximum (matches branch component sanitization)
   510→    Returns `"work"` if the result is empty.
   511→
   512→    The 31-character limit ensures worktree names match their corresponding branch
   513→    names, maintaining consistency across the worktree/branch model.
   514→
   515→    Args:
   516→        name: Arbitrary string to sanitize
   517→
   518→    Returns:
   519→        Sanitized worktree name (max 31 chars, unless timestamp already present)
   520→
   521→    Examples:
   522→        >>> sanitize_worktree_name("My_Feature")
   523→        "my-feature"
   524→        >>> sanitize_worktree_name("a" * 40)
   525→        "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"  # 31 chars
   526→        >>> sanitize_worktree_name("42-feature-01-15-1430")
   527→        "42-feature-01-15-1430"  # timestamp preserved (idempotent)
   528→    """
   529→    # If name already has a timestamp suffix, return as-is (idempotent)
   530→    if has_timestamp_suffix(name):
   531→        return name
   532→
   533→    lowered = name.strip().lower()
   534→    # Replace underscores with hyphens
   535→    replaced_underscores = lowered.replace("_", "-")
   536→    # Replace unsafe characters with hyphens
   537→    replaced = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9-]+", "-", replaced_underscores)
   538→    # Collapse consecutive hyphens
   539→    collapsed = re.sub(r"-+", "-", replaced)
   540→    # Strip leading/trailing hyphens
   541→    trimmed = collapsed.strip("-")
   542→    result = trimmed or "work"
   543→
   544→    # Truncate to 31 characters and strip trailing hyphens
   545→    if len(result) > 31:
   546→        result = result[:31].rstrip("-")
   547→
   548→    return result
   549→
   550→
   551→def sanitize_branch_component(name: str) -> str:
   552→    """Return a sanitized, predictable branch component from an arbitrary name.
   553→
   554→    - Lowercases input
   555→    - Replaces characters outside `[A-Za-z0-9_/-]` with `-`
   556→    - Collapses consecutive `-`
   557→    - Strips leading/trailing `-` and `/`
   558→    - Truncates to 31 characters maximum (matches worktree behavior)
   559→    Returns `"work"` if the result is empty.
   560→
   561→    Args:
   562→        name: Arbitrary string to sanitize
   563→
   564→    Returns:
   565→        Sanitized branch component name (max 31 chars)
   566→
   567→    Examples:
   568→        >>> sanitize_branch_component("My Feature!")
   569→        "my-feature"
   570→        >>> sanitize_branch_component("fix/bug-123")
   571→        "fix/bug-123"
   572→        >>> sanitize_branch_component("")
   573→        "work"
   574→        >>> sanitize_branch_component("a" * 40)
   575→        "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"  # 31 chars
   576→    """
   577→    lowered = name.strip().lower()
   578→    replaced = _SAFE_COMPONENT_RE.sub("-", lowered)
   579→    collapsed = re.sub(r"-+", "-", replaced)
   580→    trimmed = collapsed.strip("-/")
   581→    result = trimmed or "work"
   582→
   583→    # Truncate to 31 characters and strip trailing hyphens (matching worktree behavior)
   584→    if len(result) > 31:
   585→        result = result[:31].rstrip("-")
   586→
   587→    return result
   588→
   589→
   590→def generate_filename_from_title(title: str) -> str:
   591→    """Convert title to kebab-case filename with -plan.md suffix.
   592→
   593→    Comprehensive transformation:
   594→    1. Lowercase
   595→    2. Replace spaces with hyphens
   596→    3. Unicode normalization (NFD)
   597→    4. Remove emojis and non-alphanumeric characters (except hyphens)
   598→    5. Collapse consecutive hyphens
   599→    6. Strip leading/trailing hyphens
   600→    7. Validate at least one alphanumeric character remains
   601→    8. Append "-plan.md"
   602→
   603→    Returns "plan.md" if title is empty after cleanup.
   604→
   605→    Args:
   606→        title: Plan title to convert
   607→
   608→    Returns:
   609→        Sanitized filename ending with -plan.md
   610→
   611→    Example:
   612→        >>> generate_filename_from_title("User Auth Feature")
   613→        'user-auth-feature-plan.md'
   614→
   615→        >>> generate_filename_from_title("Fix: Bug #123")
   616→        'fix-bug-123-plan.md'
   617→
   618→        >>> generate_filename_from_title("🚀 Feature Launch 🎉")
   619→        'feature-launch-plan.md'
   620→
   621→        >>> generate_filename_from_title("café")
   622→        'cafe-plan.md'
   623→    """
   624→    # Step 1: Lowercase and strip whitespace
   625→    lowered = title.strip().lower()
   626→
   627→    # Step 2: Unicode normalization (NFD form for decomposition)
   628→    # Decompose combined characters (é → e + ´)
   629→    normalized = unicodedata.normalize("NFD", lowered)
   630→
   631→    # Step 3: Remove emojis and non-ASCII characters, convert to ASCII
   632→    # Keep only ASCII alphanumeric, spaces, and hyphens
   633→    cleaned = ""
   634→    for char in normalized:
   635→        # Keep ASCII alphanumeric, spaces, and hyphens
   636→        if ord(char) < 128 and (char.isalnum() or char in (" ", "-")):
   637→            cleaned += char
   638→        # Skip combining marks (accents) and emoji
   639→        # Skip non-ASCII characters (CJK, emoji, special symbols)
   640→
   641→    # Step 4: Replace spaces with hyphens
   642→    replaced = cleaned.replace(" ", "-")
   643→
   644→    # Step 5: Collapse consecutive hyphens
   645→    collapsed = re.sub(r"-+", "-", replaced)
   646→
   647→    # Step 6: Strip leading/trailing hyphens
   648→    trimmed = collapsed.strip("-")
   649→
   650→    # Step 7: Validate at least one alphanumeric character
   651→    if not trimmed or not any(c.isalnum() for c in trimmed):
   652→        return "plan.md"
   653→
   654→    return f"{trimmed}-plan.md"
   655→
   656→
   657→def strip_plan_from_filename(filename: str) -> str:
   658→    """Remove 'plan' or 'implementation plan' from a filename stem intelligently.
   659→
   660→    Handles case-insensitive matching and common separators (-, _, space).
   661→    If removal would leave empty string, returns original unchanged.
   662→
   663→    Args:
   664→        filename: Filename stem (without extension) to process
   665→
   666→    Returns:
   667→        Filename with plan-related words removed, or original if would be empty
   668→
   669→    Examples:
   670→        >>> strip_plan_from_filename("devclikit-extraction-plan")
   671→        "devclikit-extraction"
   672→        >>> strip_plan_from_filename("my-feature-plan")
   673→        "my-feature"
   674→        >>> strip_plan_from_filename("implementation-plan-for-auth")
   675→        "for-auth"
   676→        >>> strip_plan_from_filename("feature_implementation_plan")
   677→        "feature"
   678→        >>> strip_plan_from_filename("plan")
   679→        "plan"  # preserved - would be empty
   680→    """
   681→    original_trimmed = filename.strip("-_ \t\n\r")
   682→    original_is_plan = original_trimmed.casefold() == "plan" if original_trimmed else False
   683→
   684→    # First, handle "implementation plan" with various separators
   685→    # Pattern matches "implementation" + separator + "plan" as complete words
   686→    impl_pattern = r"(^|[-_\s])(implementation)([-_\s])(plan)([-_\s]|$)"
   687→
   688→    def replace_impl_plan(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
   689→        prefix = match.group(1)
   690→        implementation_word = match.group(2)  # Preserves original case
   691→        suffix = match.group(5)
   692→
   693→        if suffix == "" and prefix:
   694→            prefix_start = match.start(1)
   695→            preceding_segment = filename[:prefix_start]
   696→            trimmed_segment = preceding_segment.strip("-_ \t\n\r")
   697→            if trimmed_segment:
   698→                preceding_tokens = re.split(r"[-_\s]+", trimmed_segment)
   699→                if preceding_tokens:
   700→                    preceding_token = preceding_tokens[-1]
   701→                    if preceding_token.casefold() == "plan":
   702→                        return f"{prefix}{implementation_word}"
   703→
   704→        # If entire string is "implementation-plan", keep just "implementation"
   705→        if not prefix and not suffix:
   706→            return implementation_word
   707→
   708→        # If in the middle, preserve one separator
   709→        if prefix and suffix:
   710→            return prefix if prefix.strip() else suffix
   711→
   712→        # At start or end: remove it and the adjacent separator
   713→        return ""
   714→
   715→    cleaned = re.sub(impl_pattern, replace_impl_plan, filename, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
   716→
   717→    # Then handle standalone "plan" as a complete word
   718→    plan_pattern = r"(^|[-_\s])(plan)([-_\s]|$)"
   719→
   720→    def replace_plan(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
   721→        prefix = match.group(1)
   722→        suffix = match.group(3)
   723→
   724→        # If both prefix and suffix are empty (entire string is "plan"), keep it
   725→        if not prefix and not suffix:
   726→            return "plan"
   727→
   728→        # If plan is in the middle, preserve one separator
   729→        if prefix and suffix:
   730→            # Use the prefix separator if available, otherwise use suffix
   731→            return prefix if prefix.strip() else suffix
   732→
   733→        # Plan at start or end: remove it and the adjacent separator
   734→        return ""
   735→
   736→    cleaned = re.sub(plan_pattern, replace_plan, cleaned, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
   737→
   738→    def clean_separators(text: str) -> str:
   739→        stripped = text.strip("-_ \t\n\r")
   740→        stripped = re.sub(r"--+", "-", stripped)
   741→        stripped = re.sub(r"__+", "_", stripped)
   742→        stripped = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped)
   743→        return stripped
   744→
   745→    cleaned = clean_separators(cleaned)
   746→
   747→    plan_only_cleaned = clean_separators(
   748→        re.sub(plan_pattern, replace_plan, filename, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
   749→    )
   750→
   751→    if (
   752→        cleaned.casefold() == "plan"
   753→        and plan_only_cleaned
   754→        and plan_only_cleaned.casefold() != "plan"
   755→    ):
   756→        cleaned = plan_only_cleaned
   757→
   758→    # If stripping left us with nothing or just "plan", preserve original
   759→    if not cleaned or (cleaned.casefold() == "plan" and original_is_plan):
   760→        return filename
   761→
   762→    return cleaned
   763→
   764→
   765→def extract_trailing_number(name: str) -> tuple[str, int | None]:
   766→    r"""Extract trailing number from a name.
   767→
   768→    Detects trailing numbers in names using regex pattern `^(.+?)-(\d+)$`.
   769→    Returns tuple of (base_name, number) or (name, None).
   770→
   771→    Args:
   772→        name: Name to parse
   773→
   774→    Returns:
   775→        Tuple of (base_name, number) if trailing number found, else (name, None)
   776→
   777→    Examples:
   778→        >>> extract_trailing_number("my-feature")
   779→        ("my-feature", None)
   780→        >>> extract_trailing_number("my-feature-2")
   781→        ("my-feature", 2)
   782→        >>> extract_trailing_number("fix-42")
   783→        ("fix", 42)
   784→    """
   785→    match = re.match(r"^(.+?)-(\d+)$", name)
   786→    if match:
   787→        base_name = match.group(1)
   788→        number = int(match.group(2))
   789→        return (base_name, number)
   790→    return (name, None)
   791→
   792→
   793→def extract_leading_issue_number(branch_name: str) -> int | None:
   794→    """Extract leading issue number from a branch name.
   795→
   796→    Branch names follow the pattern: P{issue_number}-{slug}-{timestamp}
   797→    Examples: "P2382-convert-erk-create-raw-ext-12-05-2359"
   798→
   799→    Also supports legacy format without "P" prefix for backwards compatibility.
   800→
   801→    Args:
   802→        branch_name: Branch name to parse
   803→
   804→    Returns:
   805→        Issue number if branch starts with optional "P" followed by digits and hyphen, else None
   806→
   807→    Examples:
   808→        >>> extract_leading_issue_number("P2382-convert-erk-create-raw-ext-12-05-2359")
   809→        2382
   810→        >>> extract_leading_issue_number("P42-fix-bug")
   811→        42
   812→        >>> extract_leading_issue_number("2382-convert-erk-create-raw-ext-12-05-2359")
   813→        2382
   814→        >>> extract_leading_issue_number("42-fix-bug")
   815→        42
   816→        >>> extract_leading_issue_number("feature-branch")
   817→        None
   818→        >>> extract_leading_issue_number("master")
   819→        None
   820→    """
   821→    match = re.match(r"^[Pp]?(\d+)-", branch_name)
   822→    if match:
   823→        return int(match.group(1))
   824→    return None
   825→
   826→
   827→def extract_objective_number(branch_name: str) -> int | None:
   828→    """Extract objective number from branch name.
   829→
   830→    Supports branch naming patterns:
   831→
   832→    - Issue-based: ``P{plan}-O{objective}-{slug}-{timestamp}``
   833→    - Draft-PR (current): ``plnd/O{objective}-{slug}-{timestamp}``
   834→    - Draft-PR (legacy): ``planned/O{objective}-{slug}-{timestamp}``
   835→    - Draft-PR (legacy): ``plan/O{objective}-{slug}-{timestamp}``
   836→
   837→    Also supports lowercase "o" prefix for case-insensitive matching.
   838→
   839→    Args:
   840→        branch_name: Branch name to parse
   841→
   842→    Returns:
   843→        Objective number if branch contains O{number} after the prefix, else None
   844→
   845→    Examples:
   846→        >>> extract_objective_number("P123-O456-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430")
   847→        456
   848→        >>> extract_objective_number("P123-o456-fix-bug")
   849→        456
   850→        >>> extract_objective_number("plnd/O456-fix-auth-01-15-1430")
   851→        456
   852→        >>> extract_objective_number("planned/O456-fix-auth-01-15-1430")
   853→        456
   854→        >>> extract_objective_number("plan/O456-fix-auth-01-15-1430")
   855→        456
   856→        >>> extract_objective_number("P123-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430")
   857→        None
   858→        >>> extract_objective_number("feature-branch")
   859→        None
   860→    """
   861→    match = re.match(r"^(?:[Pp]?\d+-|pl(?:an(?:ned)?|nd)/)[Oo](\d+)-", branch_name)
   862→    if match:
   863→        return int(match.group(1))
   864→    return None
   865→
   866→
   867→def ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date(base_name: str, worktrees_dir: Path, git_ops) -> str:
   868→    """Ensure unique worktree name with datetime suffix and smart versioning.
   869→
   870→    Adds datetime suffix in format -YY-MM-DD-HHMM to the base name.
   871→    If a worktree with that name exists, increments numeric suffix starting at 2 AFTER the datetime.
   872→    Uses LBYL pattern: checks via git_ops.worktree.path_exists() before operations.
   873→
   874→    This function is used for plan-derived worktrees where multiple worktrees may be
   875→    created from the same plan, requiring datetime-based disambiguation.
   876→
   877→    Args:
   878→        base_name: Sanitized worktree base name (without datetime suffix)
   879→        worktrees_dir: Directory containing worktrees
   880→        git_ops: Git operations interface for checking path existence
   881→
   882→    Returns:
   883→        Guaranteed unique worktree name with datetime suffix
   884→
   885→    Examples:
   886→        First time: "my-feature" → "my-feature-25-11-08-1430"
   887→        Duplicate: "my-feature" → "my-feature-25-11-08-1430-2"
   888→        Next minute: "my-feature" → "my-feature-25-11-08-1431"
   889→    """
   890→    date_suffix = datetime.now().strftime(WORKTREE_DATE_SUFFIX_FORMAT)
   891→    candidate_name = f"{base_name}-{date_suffix}"
   892→
   893→    # Check if the base candidate exists
   894→    if not git_ops.worktree.path_exists(worktrees_dir / candidate_name):
   895→        return candidate_name
   896→
   897→    # Name exists, find next available number (append after date)
   898→    counter = 2
   899→    while True:
   900→        versioned_name = f"{base_name}-{date_suffix}-{counter}"
   901→        if not git_ops.worktree.path_exists(worktrees_dir / versioned_name):
   902→            return versioned_name
   903→        counter += 1
   904→
   905→
   906→def ensure_simple_worktree_name(base_name: str, worktrees_dir: Path, git_ops) -> str:
   907→    """Ensure simple worktree name without date suffix for manual checkouts.
   908→
   909→    Returns the simple name if no worktree exists at that path.
   910→    If a worktree already exists, returns the simple name (caller validates branch match).
   911→    Uses LBYL pattern: checks via git_ops.worktree.path_exists() before operations.
   912→
   913→    This function is used for manual checkout operations where predictable names are
   914→    desired (e.g., `erk co feature` → `feature` not `feature-25-11-08`).
   915→
   916→    Args:
   917→        base_name: Sanitized worktree base name
   918→        worktrees_dir: Directory containing worktrees
   919→        git_ops: Git operations interface for checking path existence
   920→
   921→    Returns:
   922→        Simple worktree name without date suffix
   923→
   924→    Examples:
   925→        First time: "my-feature" → "my-feature"
   926→        Exists: "my-feature" → "my-feature" (caller handles validation)
   927→    """
   928→    candidate_name = base_name
   929→    # Always return simple name - collision handling happens in caller
   930→    return candidate_name
   931→
   932→
   933→def ensure_unique_worktree_name(base_name: str, worktrees_dir: Path, git_ops) -> str:
   934→    """Deprecated: Use ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date for plan-derived worktrees.
   935→
   936→    This function is kept for backward compatibility but will be removed in the future.
   937→    New code should use:
   938→    - ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date() for plan-derived worktrees
   939→    - ensure_simple_worktree_name() for manual checkout operations
   940→    """
   941→    return ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date(base_name, worktrees_dir, git_ops)
   942→
   943→
   944→def default_branch_for_worktree(name: str) -> str:
   945→    """Default branch name for a worktree with the given `name`.
   946→
   947→    Returns the sanitized name directly (without any prefix).
   948→
   949→    Args:
   950→        name: Worktree name
   951→
   952→    Returns:
   953→        Default branch name (sanitized)
   954→
   955→    Examples:
   956→        >>> default_branch_for_worktree("my-feature")
   957→        "my-feature"
   958→        >>> default_branch_for_worktree("Fix Bug!")
   959→        "fix-bug"
   960→    """
   961→    return sanitize_branch_component(name)
   962→
   963→
   964→def generate_issue_branch_name(
   965→    issue_number: int | str,
   966→    title: str,
   967→    timestamp: datetime,
   968→    *,
   969→    objective_id: int | None,
   970→) -> str:
   971→    """Generate branch name for issue-based worktree.
   972→
   973→    Format: P{issue_number}-{sanitized_title}-{timestamp}
   974→    Or with objective: P{issue_number}-O{objective_id}-{sanitized_title}-{timestamp}
   975→    Example: P123-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430
   976→    Example with objective: P123-O456-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430
   977→
   978→    The branch name is constructed as:
   979→    1. P prefix + issue number + hyphen
   980→    2. If objective_id provided: O prefix + objective_id + hyphen
   981→    3. Sanitized title (lowercased, special chars replaced)
   982→    4. Truncated to 31 chars total (before timestamp)
   983→    5. Timestamp suffix appended (format: -MM-DD-HHMM)
   984→
   985→    Args:
   986→        issue_number: GitHub issue number
   987→        title: Issue title to sanitize
   988→        timestamp: Timestamp for the suffix
   989→        objective_id: Optional objective ID to encode in branch name
   990→
   991→    Returns:
   992→        Branch name in format P{num}-{slug}-{timestamp} or P{num}-O{obj}-{slug}-{timestamp}
   993→
   994→    Examples:
   995→        >>> from datetime import datetime
   996→        >>> generate_issue_branch_name(
   997→        ...     123, "Fix Auth Bug", datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30), objective_id=None
   998→        ... )
   999→        "P123-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430"
  1000→        >>> generate_issue_branch_name(
  1001→        ...     123, "Fix Auth Bug", datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30), objective_id=456
  1002→        ... )
  1003→        "P123-O456-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430"
  1004→    """
  1005→    prefix = f"P{issue_number}-"
  1006→    if objective_id is not None:
  1007→        prefix += f"O{objective_id}-"
  1008→    sanitized_title = sanitize_worktree_name(title)
  1009→    base_branch_name = (prefix + sanitized_title)[:31].rstrip("-")
  1010→    timestamp_suffix = format_branch_timestamp_suffix(timestamp)
  1011→    return base_branch_name + timestamp_suffix
  1012→
  1013→
  1014→def generate_draft_pr_branch_name(
  1015→    title: str,
  1016→    timestamp: datetime,
  1017→    *,
  1018→    objective_id: int | None,
  1019→) -> str:
  1020→    """Generate branch name for draft-PR-backed plans.
  1021→
  1022→    Format: plnd/{sanitized_title}-{timestamp}
  1023→    Or with objective: plnd/O{objective_id}-{sanitized_title}-{timestamp}
  1024→    Example: plnd/fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430
  1025→    Example with objective: plnd/O456-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430
  1026→
  1027→    No P{issue} prefix since the PR number isn't known until after creation.
  1028→
  1029→    Args:
  1030→        title: Plan title to sanitize
  1031→        timestamp: Timestamp for the suffix
  1032→        objective_id: Optional objective ID to encode in branch name
  1033→
  1034→    Returns:
  1035→        Branch name in format plnd/{slug}-{timestamp}
  1036→
  1037→    Examples:
  1038→        >>> from datetime import datetime
  1039→        >>> generate_draft_pr_branch_name(
  1040→        ...     "Fix Auth Bug", datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30), objective_id=None
  1041→        ... )
  1042→        "plnd/fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430"
  1043→        >>> generate_draft_pr_branch_name(
  1044→        ...     "Fix Auth Bug", datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30), objective_id=456
  1045→        ... )
  1046→        "plnd/O456-fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430"
  1047→    """
  1048→    prefix = "plnd/"
  1049→    if objective_id is not None:
  1050→        prefix += f"O{objective_id}-"
  1051→    sanitized_title = sanitize_worktree_name(title)
  1052→    base_branch_name = (prefix + sanitized_title)[:31].rstrip("-")
  1053→    timestamp_suffix = format_branch_timestamp_suffix(timestamp)
  1054→    return base_branch_name + timestamp_suffix
  1055→
  1056→
  1057→def derive_branch_name_from_title(title: str) -> str:
  1058→    """Derive branch name from issue/plan title.
  1059→
  1060→    This function matches the logic used by the erk-impl workflow
  1061→    to ensure CLI and workflow produce identical branch names.
  1062→
  1063→    Transforms:
  1064→    - Lowercase
  1065→    - Replace non-alphanumeric (except hyphen) with hyphen
  1066→    - Collapse consecutive hyphens
  1067→    - Strip leading/trailing hyphens
  1068→    - Truncate to 30 chars
  1069→    - Strip trailing hyphen after truncation
  1070→
  1071→    Args:
  1072→        title: Issue or plan title
  1073→
  1074→    Returns:
  1075→        Branch name (max 30 chars, kebab-case)
  1076→
  1077→    Examples:
  1078→        >>> derive_branch_name_from_title("My Feature")
  1079→        "my-feature"
  1080→        >>> derive_branch_name_from_title("Fix Bug #123!")
  1081→        "fix-bug-123"
  1082→        >>> derive_branch_name_from_title("A" * 40)
  1083→        "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"  # 30 chars (one less than sanitize_branch_component)
  1084→    """
  1085→    # Convert to lowercase
  1086→    branch_name = title.lower()
  1087→    # Replace non-alphanumeric (except hyphen) with hyphen
  1088→    branch_name = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9-]", "-", branch_name)
  1089→    # Collapse consecutive hyphens
  1090→    branch_name = re.sub(r"-+", "-", branch_name)
  1091→    # Strip leading/trailing hyphens
  1092→    branch_name = branch_name.strip("-")
  1093→
  1094→    # Truncate to 30 chars (matches workflow logic)
  1095→    branch_name = branch_name[:30]
  1096→
  1097→    # Strip trailing hyphen after truncation
  1098→    branch_name = branch_name.rstrip("-")
  1099→
  1100→    return branch_name
  1101→
